note: WALL-CLOCK validation of the E5 analytic flop win. Multifrontal (JunctionTreeCholesky::solve) vs dense Gaussian (InfoEvolventHead::solve), identical answer (E5). Measured speedup GROWS exactly as the O(d·w^3) vs O(d^3) asymptotic predicts (5.9x->255x over d 105->889), but is ~5x BELOW the analytic flop count -- the dense baseline is contiguous/cache-friendly, the multifrontal has per-clique small-Vec allocation overhead. Flop count is the CEILING the contiguous-storage rewrite would approach. At d=889: 85us vs 21.75ms (online-viable). Dense baseline is Gaussian elim (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) so ratio is a mild over-estimate of the pure win.
